name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-106
title: prompt-threshold set via rabbit-config does not trigger policy-refresh banner
description:
## Repro

1. In a fresh session, run:
   `python3 .claude/features/rabbit-config/skills/rabbit-config/scripts/rabbit-config.py prompt-threshold set 10`
   Tool reports: `OK: .claude/settings.local.json::env.RABBIT_REFRESH_EVERY set`

2. Continue the session, entering well over 10 user prompts.

## Expected

After every 10th user prompt, the UserPromptSubmit dispatcher should invoke `rabbit-cage.check_counter_threshold_refresh` (declared in rabbit-cage/feature.json runtime.UserPromptSubmit), which should print a [rabbit] policy-refresh banner / inject the policy block back into context.

## Actual

No [rabbit] policy-refresh banner appears at any point during the session, even after entering 10+ prompts. Refresh appears not to fire at all.

## Possible causes (to investigate during work-the-bug phase)

- The runtime API may not read `RABBIT_REFRESH_EVERY` from `.claude/settings.local.json::env` at all; it may only read from process env, in which case setting it via rabbit-config has no effect on an already-running session and possibly not on new sessions either.
- The counter file the API checks may not be incrementing on each UserPromptSubmit event.
- The UserPromptSubmit dispatcher may not be wired to surface `print`/`banner` partition outputs from this particular API.
- The user-prompt-submit-dispatcher.py hook may not be registered or matched correctly in settings.json.

## Suggested test gap

A behavioural test that sets RABBIT_REFRESH_EVERY (via the same mechanism the user used: rabbit-config script), simulates N UserPromptSubmit invocations, and asserts the refresh banner is emitted on the Nth prompt. The configurable surface and the runtime behaviour must be tested together — not just the script that writes to settings.local.json.

history.note: TDD cycle complete: refresh banner emission landed in contract e2acdf7d alongside spec a400f98e; drift unblocker filed as CONTRACT-BUG-46 (6cf4f724)
